---
title: "US Gas Prices Trend Analysis"
subtitle: "INFO 526 - Summer 2026 - Final Project"
author: "Zachary Zhicheng Xu"
title-slide-attributes:
  data-background-image: images/watercolour_sys02_img34_teacup-ocean.jpg
  data-background-size: stretch
  data-background-opacity: "0.7"
  data-slide-number: none
format:
  revealjs:
    theme:  ['data/customtheming.scss']
  
editor: visual
execute:
  echo: false
---


::: {.cell}

:::


## Motivation

Gasoline is important in our lives, especially in states like Arizona, where people rely on personal vehicles for daily transportation. However, in recent years, especially after the COVID-19 Pandemic, the price of gasoline has changed almost day by day, which has made me sensitive to the gasoline price. When 2026 started, the regular gasoline price in the Greater Phoenix Area was just around \$3, but after the Iran War started, the regular gasoline price increased to around \$4.5 in a couple of days. For this reason, I plan to do the US Gas Prices Trend Analysis.

# Data


::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}

```
# A tibble: 22,360 × 5
   date       fuel     grade   formulation  price
   <date>     <chr>    <chr>   <chr>        <dbl>
 1 1990-08-20 gasoline regular all           1.19
 2 1990-08-20 gasoline regular conventional  1.19
 3 1990-08-27 gasoline regular all           1.25
 4 1990-08-27 gasoline regular conventional  1.25
 5 1990-09-03 gasoline regular all           1.24
 6 1990-09-03 gasoline regular conventional  1.24
 7 1990-09-10 gasoline regular all           1.25
 8 1990-09-10 gasoline regular conventional  1.25
 9 1990-09-17 gasoline regular all           1.27
10 1990-09-17 gasoline regular conventional  1.27
# ℹ 22,350 more rows
```


:::
:::


Source: 'https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/main/data/2025/2025-07-01/weekly_gas_prices.csv' from U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA).

# Question 1

How did US Fuel prices change during major events between 2002 and 2025? (the Iraq War 2003, the Global Financial Crisis 2008, and the COVID-19 Pandemic 2020-2022.)

# Major Events

- The Iraq War (2003)
- The 2008 Global Financial Crisis (2007-2009)
- The COVID-19 Pandemic (2020-2023)


::: {.cell}

:::


## Compare Gasoline Price by Grade


::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](presentation_files/figure-revealjs/1-compare-gasoline-1.png){width=960}
:::
:::


## Gasoline and Diesel Prices during Major Events


::: {.cell}

:::



::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](presentation_files/figure-revealjs/fuel_compare-plot-1.png){width=1440}
:::
:::


## U.S. Fuel Prices by Fuel Grade


::: {.cell}

:::



::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](presentation_files/figure-revealjs/1-all-plot-1.png){width=1440}
:::
:::


## Conclusion

- 

  1.  All grades(gasoline and diesel) have similar long-term price trends.

- 

  2.  The gasoline and diesel prices would increase a lot during the major events. The price would reach the highest point in the middle of the major events, and then would decrease and return to the prices before the major events happened.

- 

  3.  The price trends for all the major events are similar.

- 

  4.  The major events can strongly influence the gasoline and diesel prices.

- 

  5.  It seems like the influence is limited during the major events period; once the events end, the price will be back to normal.

# Question 2

How do fuel grade and formulation influence gasoline prices, and how the price differences changed over time?

## Regular and Midgrade Price Difference


::: {.cell}

:::



::: {.cell}

:::



::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](presentation_files/figure-revealjs/2-reg-mid-1.png){width=960}
:::
:::


## Regular and Premium Price Difference


::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](presentation_files/figure-revealjs/2-reg-pre-1.png){width=960}
:::
:::


## Midgrade and Premium Price Difference


::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](presentation_files/figure-revealjs/2-mid-pre-1.png){width=960}
:::
:::


## Distribution of Gasoline Prices by Grade


::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](presentation_files/figure-revealjs/2-dis-gas-grade-1.png){width=960}
:::
:::


## Price Difference between Reformulated and Conventional Gasoline (Ref. minus Con.)


::: {.cell}
::: {.cell-output-display}
![](presentation_files/figure-revealjs/2-form-price-diff-plot-1.png){width=960}
:::
:::


## Conclusion

- 

  1.  The difference between regular and other higher-grade gasoline is increasing year by year.

- 

  2.  Higher-grade gasolines have the highest outliers.

- 

  3.  In the long-term trend, the reformed gasoline becomes more expensive than the conventional gasoline.

- 

  4.  The vehicles that use higher grade gasoline will spend more and more money in the long-term trend; the vehicles that use regular grade gasoline have more cost-effectiveness in the long-term.

# Thank you for watching my presentation!

<script>
  // htmlwidgets need to know to resize themselves when slides are shown/hidden.
  // Fire the "slideenter" event (handled by htmlwidgets.js) when the current
  // slide changes (different for each slide format).
  (function () {
    // dispatch for htmlwidgets
    function fireSlideEnter() {
      const event = window.document.createEvent("Event");
      event.initEvent("slideenter", true, true);
      window.document.dispatchEvent(event);
    }

    function fireSlideChanged(previousSlide, currentSlide) {
      fireSlideEnter();

      // dispatch for shiny
      if (window.jQuery) {
        if (previousSlide) {
          window.jQuery(previousSlide).trigger("hidden");
        }
        if (currentSlide) {
          window.jQuery(currentSlide).trigger("shown");
        }
      }
    }

    // hookup for slidy
    if (window.w3c_slidy) {
      window.w3c_slidy.add_observer(function (slide_num) {
        // slide_num starts at position 1
        fireSlideChanged(null, w3c_slidy.slides[slide_num - 1]);
      });
    }

  })();
</script>
